tasks.instruction: Read two articles about probiotic foods and create a personal note titled "probiotic benefits" with at least four bullet points summarizing at least two key points from each article.

target_names: Probiotic Foods: A Beginner’s Guide to Feeding Your Gut
Probiotic Foods for Sensitive Guts and IBS

bullet_points: Probiotic foods contain live and active cultures that can help support a balanced gut microbiome.
Common probiotic foods include yogurt with live cultures, kefir, sauerkraut, kimchi, miso, tempeh, and kombucha.
Gentler probiotic foods for sensitive guts include lactose-free yogurt with live cultures, firm tempeh, certain low-FODMAP kimchi or sauerkraut, and some fermented hard cheeses.
For some people with IBS, probiotic foods may reduce bloating frequency, support regular bowel movements, and decrease antibiotic-associated diarrhea.
